```mermaid
mindmap
  root(Light – Reflection and Refraction)
    ::icon(fa fa-lightbulb)
    Light
      Visible Objects
        Object reflects light
        Reflected light received by eyes
      Transparent Medium
        Light transmitted through
      Phenomena Associated with Light
        Image formation by mirrors
        Twinkling of stars
        Rainbow colours
        Bending of light (Refraction)
      Propagation of Light
        Travels in straight lines (Ray Optics)
          Sharp shadows of opaque objects
        Diffraction (Bending around small obstacles)
          Wave nature of light (Higher classes)
        Interaction with Matter
          Particle nature of light (Quantum Theory)
          Modern Quantum Theory (Reconciles wave and particle)
    Reflection of Light
      ::icon(fa fa-mirror)
      Definition
        Highly polished surfaces (e.g., mirror) reflect most light
      Laws of Reflection
        (i) Angle of Incidence = Angle of Reflection
        (ii) Incident Ray, Normal, Reflected Ray lie in the same plane
      Application
        Image Formation by Plane Mirrors
          Properties of Image
            Virtual
            Erect
            Size = Object Size
            Image distance = Object distance (behind mirror)
            Laterally Inverted
    Spherical Mirrors
      ::icon(fa fa-camera-retro)
      Definition
        Reflecting surface is a part of a sphere
      Types of Spherical Mirrors
        Concave Mirror
          Reflecting surface curved inwards (faces towards center of sphere)
          Example: Inner surface of a shining spoon
        Convex Mirror
          Reflecting surface curved outwards
          Example: Outer surface of a shining spoon
      Terms Associated with Spherical Mirrors (Introductory)
        (Details to be covered in further study)
    Refraction of Light
      ::icon(fa fa-tint)
      Definition
        Bending of light as it passes from one medium to another
      (Further details to be covered)
```
